name: Jira Team Status
description: Show a team's Jira sprint or backlog scored for agent-readiness, then pick up a story to deliver or shape. Scores each story against the five-question actionability bar (self-contained change, reachable repo scope, binary ACs, no mid-flight decision, right-sized for one PR), groups Tier A stories by complexity (Quick / Standard / Involved), and surfaces a pick-up hand-off: start delivery on a ready story (routes to jira-defect-flow for bugs or new-spec for tasks/stories) or shape a blocked story into an executable form (collaborative rewrite + optional update-issue with explicit consent). Session-entry-point pattern; reads Jira for the snapshot and may write once in the shaping hand-off with explicit user confirmation.

deps._runtime_contract: Sibling skills are addressed BY NAME at runtime. The agent's harness resolves the name to whatever install location the user picked. `source` below is install guidance only — never a runtime path.

skills.name: jira
skills.purpose: All Jira reads for the snapshot (check, search) and the conditional update-issue call in the shaping hand-off (only with explicit user consent). Hard dependency — there is no degraded path without it.

skills.name: jira-defect-flow
skills.purpose: Pick-up hand-off for Tier A stories whose issuetype is Bug or Defect. Soft dependency — when absent, the skill surfaces an install hint rather than stopping.

skills.name: new-spec
skills.purpose: Pick-up hand-off for Tier A stories whose issuetype is Story or Task. Soft dependency — when absent, the skill surfaces an install hint rather than stopping.

input.arguments: A Jira project key (required). Optional: sprint name or ID (default: open sprints), team name or JQL filter. Optionally a repo URL or name for grounding.

artifacts: A scored sprint snapshot with four sections: §1 Agent-ready (Tier A, grouped by Quick/Standard/Involved), §2 Parallel batching candidates, §3 Gated (Tier B), §4 Needs shaping (Tier C + Blocked). Followed by a pick-up hand-off offering delivery routing or shaping. Conditional Jira write (update-issue) with explicit user consent in the shaping flow.
